forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,31 @@
+---
+depends_on:
+- 01KXHBA11VYJVDH30ACJX2APM3
+- 01KXHBBA1PN5PQJVEVHCQTBQ9T
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: 8b80
+title: Role protocols + AgentSideConnection / ClientSideConnection
+---
+## What
+Hand-write the two role protocols and the symmetric connection objects (spec §4) in `Sources/FoundationModelsACP/Connection/`:
+
+- `Agent` protocol: `initialize`, `newSession`, `loadSession`, `prompt` (returns `PromptResponse` carrying `StopReason`), `cancel` (notification), `authenticate`, `setSessionConfigOption`, deprecated `setSessionMode` (with `@available(*, deprecated)`), plus stabilized session management: `listSessions`, `resumeSession`, `deleteSession`, `closeSession`, `logout`.
+- `Client` protocol: `sessionUpdate` (notification), `requestPermission`, `readTextFile`, `writeTextFile`, and terminals: `createTerminal`, `terminalOutput`, `waitForTerminalExit`, `killTerminal`, `releaseTerminal`.
+- `AgentSideConnection(stream:) { conn in MyAgent(conn) }` and `ClientSideConnection(stream:) { agent in MyClient(agent) }` — each takes a **factory closure**, not a finished handler, so the handler captures its own connection and can issue reverse-direction calls (without this an `Agent` has no handle to fire `sessionUpdate`/`requestPermission`).
+- Dispatch uses the **generated routing table** exclusively — no hand-typed method strings.
+- Optional methods are gated by advertised capabilities (`clientCapabilities.terminal`, `fileSystem`, provider hooks); unsupported calls answer JSON-RPC -32601 method-not-found.
+- Default protocol implementations for optional methods throw method-not-found so conformers implement only what they support.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+- [ ] An `Agent` impl wired via `AgentSideConnection` receives `initialize`/`newSession`/`prompt` and can fire `sessionUpdate` + `requestPermission` back through the `conn` handed to its factory
+- [ ] A `Client` impl wired via `ClientSideConnection` drives the agent and receives its reverse calls
+- [ ] Calling a capability-gated method the peer didn't advertise yields `RequestError` code -32601
+- [ ] `setSessionMode` emits a deprecation warning at compile time
+
+## Tests
+- [ ] `Tests/FoundationModelsACPTests/RoleDispatchTests.swift` — over `InMemoryTransport`: full method matrix dispatches to the right handler with decoded params; unknown/ungated methods → -32601
+- [ ] `Tests/FoundationModelsACPTests/FactoryClosureTests.swift` — agent factory captures conn and successfully issues a reverse `requestPermission` during a `prompt`
+- [ ] Run `swift test` — exits 0
+
+## Workflow
+- Use `/tdd` — write failing tests first, then implement to make them pass.
\ No newline at end of file

-- `Client` protocol: `sessionUpdate` (notification), `requestPermission`, `readTextFile`, `writeTextFile`, and terminals: `createTerminal`, `terminalOutput`, `waitForTerminalExit`, `killTerminal`, `releaseTerminal`.
-- `AgentSideConnection(stream:) { conn in MyAgent(conn) }` and `ClientSideConnection(stream:) { agent in MyClient(agent) }` — each takes a **factory closure**, not a finished handler, so the handler captures its own connection and can issue reverse-direction calls (without this an `Agent` has no handle to fire `sessionUpdate`/`requestPermission`).
-- Dispatch uses the **generated routing table** exclusively — no hand-typed method strings.
-- Optional methods are gated by advertised capabilities (`clientCapabilities.terminal`, `fileSystem`, provider hooks); unsupported calls answer JSON-RPC -32601 method-not-found.
-- Default protocol implementations for optional methods throw method-not-found so conformers implement only what they support.

-- [ ] A `Client` impl wired via `ClientSideConnection` drives the agent and receives its reverse calls
-- [ ] Calling a capability-gated method the peer didn't advertise yields `RequestError` code -32601
-- [ ] `setSessionMode` emits a deprecation warning at compile time

-- [ ] Run `swift test` — exits 0
-
-## Workflow
-- Use `/tdd` — write failing tests first, then implement to make them pass.
\ No newline at end of file

forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -1,4 +1,21 @@
 ---
+comments:
+- actor: wballard
+  id: 01kxk7z5jg3z24d5m42x2ypkwz
+  text: |-
+    Picked up. Research complete across the Connection actor (^tbq9t), routing-table (^t9qwxj), and full-surface (^x2apm3) threads.
+
+    DESIGN (Sources/FoundationModelsACP/Connection/):
+    - Agent + Client protocols hand-written per spec §4. Required methods (initialize/newSession/prompt/cancel for Agent; sessionUpdate/requestPermission for Client) have no defaults; all OPTIONAL methods get default protocol-extension impls that `throw RequestError.methodNotFound(<wire>)` — this IS the capability gate (implement only what your advertised caps cover). setSessionMode carries `@available(*, deprecated, message:)` on the protocol requirement so callers get a compile-time warning.
+    - Two connection wrappers `final class ... : Sendable`, each wrapping one `Connection` actor:
+      - AgentSideConnection(stream:) { conn in MyAgent(conn) } — serves inbound Client→Agent calls by dispatching to the Agent; exposes outbound Agent→Client calls (sessionUpdate/requestPermission/fs*/terminal*) as its own methods (the `conn` handed to the factory).
+      - ClientSideConnection(stream:) { agent in MyClient(agent) } — serves inbound Agent→Client calls by dispatching to the Client; exposes outbound Client→Agent calls (initialize/newSession/prompt/...) as its own methods.
+    - Init cycle broken with a write-once role holder: the Connection request/notification handlers capture the holder box (a Mutex-backed cell), NOT self; Connection.init is awaited, then factory(self) runs synchronously before any read-loop suspension, so the role is set before the first inbound dispatch. Proven safe: no await between Connection.init returning and factory() completing.
+    - Dispatch driven by ACPMethodTable: look up MethodInfo by (side, wireMethod); unknown wire method for the served side → -32601; kind mismatch handled by the request vs notification split. The typed decode+call switches on info.handlerName (Swift-side identifiers), so WIRE method strings live ONLY in the generated table. Outbound methods resolve their wire name from the table by handler name too — zero hand-typed wire strings in the Connection layer.
+    - Params decode JSONValue→typed via JSONEncoder/JSONDecoder bridge; decode failure → -32602 invalidParams. Result encodes typed→JSONValue. _meta rides through as opaque JSONValue on the models.
+
+    TDD: RoleDispatchTests + FactoryClosureTests RED first, then implement to green.
+  timestamp: 2026-07-15T15:57:45.424686+00:00
 depends_on:
 - 01KXHBA11VYJVDH30ACJX2APM3
 - 01KXHBBA1PN5PQJVEVHCQTBQ9T

forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -34,15 +34,33 @@
 - Default protocol implementations for optional methods throw method-not-found so conformers implement only what they support.

 ## Acceptance Criteria
-- [ ] An `Agent` impl wired via `AgentSideConnection` receives `initialize`/`newSession`/`prompt` and can fire `sessionUpdate` + `requestPermission` back through the `conn` handed to its factory
-- [ ] A `Client` impl wired via `ClientSideConnection` drives the agent and receives its reverse calls
-- [ ] Calling a capability-gated method the peer didn't advertise yields `RequestError` code -32601
-- [ ] `setSessionMode` emits a deprecation warning at compile time
+- [x] An `Agent` impl wired via `AgentSideConnection` receives `initialize`/`newSession`/`prompt` and can fire `sessionUpdate` + `requestPermission` back through the `conn` handed to its factory
+- [x] A `Client` impl wired via `ClientSideConnection` drives the agent and receives its reverse calls
+- [x] Calling a capability-gated method the peer didn't advertise yields `RequestError` code -32601
+- [x] `setSessionMode` emits a deprecation warning at compile time

 ## Tests
-- [ ] `Tests/FoundationModelsACPTests/RoleDispatchTests.swift` — over `InMemoryTransport`: full method matrix dispatches to the right handler with decoded params; unknown/ungated methods → -32601
-- [ ] `Tests/FoundationModelsACPTests/FactoryClosureTests.swift` — agent factory captures conn and successfully issues a reverse `requestPermission` during a `prompt`
-- [ ] Run `swift test` — exits 0
+- [x] `Tests/FoundationModelsACPTests/RoleDispatchTests.swift` — over `InMemoryTransport`: full method matrix dispatches to the right handler with decoded params; unknown/ungated methods → -32601
+- [x] `Tests/FoundationModelsACPTests/FactoryClosureTests.swift` — agent factory captures conn and successfully issues a reverse `requestPermission` during a `prompt`
+- [x] Run `swift test` — exits 0

 ## Workflow
-- Use `/tdd` — write failing tests first, then implement to make them pass.
\ No newline at end of file
+- Use `/tdd` — write failing tests first, then implement to make them pass.
+
+## Review Findings (2026-07-15 11:24)
+
+- [ ] `Sources/FoundationModelsACP/Connection/Agent.swift:76` — Nine default implementations (loadSession, authenticate, setSessionConfigOption, setSessionMode, listSessions, resumeSession, deleteSession, closeSession, logout) contain verbatim copies of the same error-throwing logic, differing only in the handler string parameter. This pattern is repeated at lines 76, 80, 84, 90, 96, 100, 104, 108, 112. Extract a helper method that takes the handler name as a parameter, e.g., `private func methodNotFound(handler: String) throws -> Never { throw RequestError.methodNotFound(RoleRouting.wire(handler: handler, on: .agent)) }`, and have each default implementation call it: `public func loadSession(...) { try methodNotFound(handler: "loadSession") }`.
+- [ ] `Sources/FoundationModelsACP/Connection/AgentSideConnection.swift:79` — Within the dispatch method, eight switch cases (initialize, newSession, loadSession, prompt, authenticate, setSessionConfigOption, listSessions, resumeSession) are near-verbatim copies, each calling `RoleDispatch.serveResult` with identical structure, differing only in handler name and request/response types. Refactor dispatch to be table-driven, using the ACPMethodTable routing information to eliminate the repetitive switch cases and route dynamically based on handler metadata.
+- [ ] `Sources/FoundationModelsACP/Connection/AgentSideConnection.swift:100` — Within the dispatch method, three switch cases (deleteSession, closeSession, logout) are near-verbatim copies, each calling `RoleDispatch.serveEmpty` with identical structure, differing only in handler name and request type. Refactor dispatch to be table-driven using ACPMethodTable routing information to eliminate the repetitive switch cases.
+- [ ] `Sources/FoundationModelsACP/Connection/AgentSideConnection.swift:165` — Five outbound methods (requestPermission, readTextFile, createTerminal, terminalOutput, waitForTerminalExit) contain near-verbatim copies of the same RoleDispatch.callResult pattern, differing only in method name, parameter type, return type, and handler string. Create a generic helper that encapsulates the callResult pattern and accepts type and handler parameters, eliminating the repetitive boilerplate.
+- [ ] `Sources/FoundationModelsACP/Connection/AgentSideConnection.swift:191` — Three outbound methods (writeTextFile, killTerminal, releaseTerminal) contain near-verbatim copies of the same RoleDispatch.callEmpty pattern, differing only in method name, parameter type, and handler string. Create a generic helper that encapsulates the callEmpty pattern and accepts parameter type and handler parameters, eliminating the repetitive boilerplate.
+- [ ] `Sources/FoundationModelsACP/Connection/Client.swift:56` — Seven default implementations (readTextFile, writeTextFile, createTerminal, terminalOutput, waitForTerminalExit, killTerminal, releaseTerminal) contain verbatim copies of the same error-throwing logic, differing only in the handler string parameter. This pattern is repeated at lines 56, 60, 64, 68, 72, 76, 80. Extract a helper method that takes the handler name as a parameter, e.g., `private func methodNotFound(handler: String) throws -> Never { throw RequestError.methodNotFound(RoleRouting.wire(handler: handler, on: .client)) }`, and have each default implementation call it.
+- [ ] `Sources/FoundationModelsACP/Connection/ClientSideConnection.swift:27` — The init method in ClientSideConnection (lines 27–46) is a near-verbatim copy of AgentSideConnection.init, differing only in class name, factory type parameter, holder generic type, and closure parameter names. Extract a generic helper function or use a generic connection wrapper class that both AgentSideConnection and ClientSideConnection can delegate to, parameterized by role type, to eliminate the structural duplication.
+- [ ] `Sources/FoundationModelsACP/Connection/ClientSideConnection.swift:62` — The `serveRequest` method in ClientSideConnection (lines 62–69) is a near-verbatim copy of AgentSideConnection.serveRequest, differing only in role parameter type (`Client` vs `Agent`) and variable names. Extract a generic `serveRequest<Role: Sendable>` helper that accepts a `RoleHolder<any Role>` and a dispatch closure, eliminating the copy.
+- [ ] `Sources/FoundationModelsACP/Connection/ClientSideConnection.swift:79` — Within the dispatch method, five switch cases (requestPermission, readTextFile, createTerminal, terminalOutput, waitForTerminalExit) are near-verbatim copies, each calling `RoleDispatch.serveResult` with identical structure, differing only in handler name and request/response types. Refactor dispatch to be table-driven using ACPMethodTable routing information to eliminate the repetitive switch cases.
+- [ ] `Sources/FoundationModelsACP/Connection/ClientSideConnection.swift:96` — Within the dispatch method, three switch cases (writeTextFile, killTerminal, releaseTerminal) are near-verbatim copies, each calling `RoleDispatch.serveEmpty` with identical structure, differing only in handler name and request type. Refactor dispatch to be table-driven using ACPMethodTable routing information to eliminate the repetitive switch cases.
+- [ ] `Sources/FoundationModelsACP/Connection/ClientSideConnection.swift:133` — The `serveNotification` method in ClientSideConnection (lines 133–145) is a near-verbatim copy of AgentSideConnection.serveNotification, differing only in role parameter type, handler case name (`sessionUpdate` vs `sessionCancel`), and the notification type/method invoked. Extract a generic `serveNotification<Role: Sendable>` helper that accepts the role type and a handler-dispatch closure, eliminating the structural copy.
+- [ ] `Sources/FoundationModelsACP/Connection/ClientSideConnection.swift:154` — Nine outbound methods (initialize, newSession, loadSession, prompt, authenticate, setSessionConfigOption, setSessionMode, listSessions, resumeSession) contain near-verbatim copies of the same RoleDispatch.callResult pattern, differing only in method name, parameter type, return type, and handler string. Create a generic helper that encapsulates the callResult pattern and accepts type and handler parameters, eliminating the repetitive boilerplate.
+- [ ] `Sources/FoundationModelsACP/Connection/ClientSideConnection.swift:274` — Three outbound methods (deleteSession, closeSession, logout) contain near-verbatim copies of the same RoleDispatch.callEmpty pattern, differing only in method name, parameter type, and handler string. Create a generic helper that encapsulates the callEmpty pattern and accepts parameter type and handler parameters, eliminating the repetitive boilerplate.
+- [ ] `Sources/FoundationModelsACP/Connection/ClientSideConnection.swift:307` — The close() method in ClientSideConnection (lines 307–309) is a verbatim copy of AgentSideConnection.close() (lines 260–262), including the documentation comment. Extract the close() method to a shared location or base class/protocol, or consolidate both connection classes through a generic wrapper to avoid the duplication.
+- [ ] `Tests/FoundationModelsACPTests/RoleTestSupport.swift:287` — wireMethod() reinvents the handler-to-wire-name lookup that RoleRouting.wire() in RoleDispatch already provides, duplicating identical ACPMethodTable search logic. Make RoleRouting.wire() public (it's already needed by tests for the routing-table-level assertions), or create a public test helper in the main module that tests can import instead of reimplementing.
\ No newline at end of file

--- original

     TDD: RoleDispatchTests + FactoryClosureTests RED first, then implement to green.
   timestamp: 2026-07-15T15:57:45.424686+00:00
+- actor: wballard
+  id: 01kxkafacj9de2yjjyqensw4qw
+  text: |-
+    Iteration 2: implementation landed (double-check PASS), then review pass 1 (checkpoint 3dcba95) returned 15 findings, all duplication class. Fixed at root by class:
+
+    - Default-impl duplication (Agent.swift, Client.swift): extracted a `private func unsupported(_:) throws -> Never` helper in each protocol extension; every optional default is now `try unsupported("handlerName")`. Resolves the two default-impl findings (16 sub-instances).
+    - Cross-connection machinery duplication (init / serveRequest / serveNotification / close, flagged 4x across ClientSideConnection): extracted a generic `RoleConnectionCore<Role: Sendable>` (new file) that owns the Connection + write-once RoleHolder, does the served-table guard once, and takes role-specific `dispatchRequest`/`dispatchNotification` closures. Both connections now delegate; the init-cycle break, guard, and shutdown live in one place. This is exactly the "generic connection wrapper parameterized by role type" the findings recommended.
+    - Outbound-method boilerplate (flagged 4x): each connection now has private `call`/`callEmpty`/`notify` helpers that fix `connection` + `peerSide`; every outbound public method is a one-line delegation (`try await call("x", params, returning: T.self)`) — the generic helper the findings asked for.
+    - Test helper `wireMethod` reinventing `RoleRouting.wire` (RoleTestSupport.swift): deleted it; RoleDispatchTests now uses `@testable import` and calls the production `RoleRouting.wire(handler:on:)` directly. Reuse over reinvention, no public-API leak.
+
+    DISPATCH-SWITCH findings (AgentSideConnection.serve, ClientSideConnection.serve — the serveResult/serveEmpty case groups): the shared decode/call/encode logic is already extracted into `RoleDispatch.serveResult`/`serveEmpty`, and each arm is a single delegating call. The findings' recommended remedy — a runtime table over `ACPMethodTable` metadata — cannot be satisfied: `MethodInfo.paramsTypeName` is a runtime `String`, and Swift has no way to reify it back into the static type needed to decode `params` into the correct model and pass it to the typed role method. A dictionary of typed closures keyed by handler name has identical per-entry structure (no reduction) and loses compile-time exhaustiveness. This is the irreducible type-safe wire→typed binding; documented in the `serve` docstrings. Every wire method string still lives only in the generated `ACPMethodTable` (grep of Connection/ for `session/|fs/|terminal/|$/` = zero matches).
+
+    Verification: swift build 0 warnings; swift test 100 FoundationModelsACPTests + 108 ACPGenerateTests = 208 pass, 0 warnings. Committing checkpoint, then review pass 2 on HEAD~1..HEAD.
